steps.title: Check current gitea setup
steps.description:
Verify your gitea version and configuration:
```bash
gitea --version
```
This feature requires a working gitea installation.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Contributors graph
steps.description:
**Let's start by taking apart existing solutions to identify required data and possible data structure.**

# GitHub

API endpoint for contibutions data is `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/graphs/contributors-data`.

The returned JSON data is basically a list of objects (each representing one
```yaml
[
  { ... }, // User with least contributions
    ...
  { ... }, // User with second most contributions
  { ... }  // User with most contributions
]
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Contributors graph" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
**Let's start by taking apart existing solutions to identify required data and possible data structure.**

# GitHub

API endpoint for contibutions data is `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/graphs/contributors-data`.

The returned JSON data is basically a list of objects (each representing one contributor) sorted least contributions first, most contributions last:
```
[
  { ...
codeSnippets:
[
  { ... }, // User with least contributions
    ...
  { ... }, // User with second most contributions
  { ... }  // User with most contributions
]

[
  { ... }, // Latest commit
  { ... }, // Second latest commit
    ...
  { ... }, // First commit
]
